summary: Product owner clarified that the blocker is not map shape polish but the hidden spatial topology: a regular rectangular, hex, sequential-id, or lattice substrate disguised with irregular polygons is unacceptable. Mature strategy-game topology research must be incorporated before the client task proceeds.

decisions.decision: A renderer that visually disguises a regular grid or lattice as irregular regions does not satisfy M7 map topology recovery.
Pause client implementation review until a gameplay_designer completes mature-strategy-game topology research and lead forwards the resulting constraints to the client writer.

risks.summary: Screenshots alone can falsely pass if visual regions look irregular while pathing, selection, adjacency, or route explanations still derive from hidden grid/lattice logic.

requested_action: Interrupt client_engineer, spawn a read-only gameplay_designer research pass on mature strategy-game map topology, consult the visual design thread for readability constraints, then resume client work only with hard anti-grid implementation and test constraints.
